title: "figures_adap"
output: html_document
date: "2026-01-09"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
# run after all the datasets and models throuhg fig 4 have been run
# save.image("~/eiee/capable/2.2_survey/wave2/core/mitig_adap_paper_data.RData")

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
setwd("C:/Users/Sanford/Documents/eiee/capable/2.2_survey/wave2/core")

### Packages
pacman::p_load(dplyr, stringr, tidyr, haven, ggplot2, ggimage, broom, kableExtra, stargazer, forcats, patchwork, srvyr, ggsci, rio, ggpattern, lubridate, knitr, psych,showtext, ggtext, relaimpo,purrr)
library(tidyverse)   # ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, forcats, etc.
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(modelsummary)
library(RCT)
library(survey)

#font_add_google("Noto Sans", "notosans")
#showtext_auto()

```

```{r load_data}


w1 <- read.csv("C:/Users/Sanford/Documents/eiee/capable/2.2_survey/wave1/core/clean_w1_data_groups.csv")
nrow(w1)
w1$wave = 1 

w2 <- read.csv("C:/Users/Sanford/Documents/eiee/capable/2.2_survey/wave2/core/wave2_core_clean_withweights.csv")
#w2_check <- read.csv("C:/Users/Sanford/Documents/eiee/capable/2.2_survey/wave2/core/wave2_core_clean_withweights.csv")
nrow(w2)
w2$wave = 2 

# table(w2$male_female)
# table(w2$quota_gndr)
# table(w2$quota_educ_category)
# table(w2$cntry)
# table(w2$attn_check_overall)


#w2$cntry[w2$cntry == "country name in english"] <- "Poland"

# make cntry variable consistent
country_map <- c("France" = "FR", "Germany" = "DE", "Greece" = "GR", 
                 "Hungary" = "HU", "Italy" = "IT", "The Netherlands" = "NL", 
                 "Poland" = "PL", "Slovenia" = "SI", "Spain" = "ES", 
                 "Lithuania" = "LT")

# Replace country names with abbreviations
w2 <- w2 %>%
  mutate(cntry = country_map[cntry])
table(w2$cntry)

# Drop countries with no adaptation data: SE, DK, AT, CZ, LT
drop <- c("SE","DK","AT","CZ", 'LT')

table(w1$cntry)

w1 <- w1 %>% 
  filter(! cntry %in% drop)

w2 <- w2 %>% 
  filter(! cntry %in% drop)

table(w2_check$cntry,useNA = 'always')
table(w1$cntry,useNA = 'always')

# Fix income for both waves 
w1$hh_inc[w1$hh_inc == -99] <- NA 
w1$hh_inc[w1$hh_inc == -98] <- NA 

w2$hh_inc[w2$hh_inc == -99] <- NA 
w2$hh_inc[w2$hh_inc == -98] <- NA 

nrow(w1)+nrow(w2)

# W2 education needs fixing 
w2 <- w2 %>%
  mutate(
    quota_group_educ = case_when(
      
      # Prefer not to answer to NA
      quota_educ < 0 ~ NA, 
      
      # System 1
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ == 1 ~ 1, 
      
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ == 2 ~ 2, 
      
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ == 3 ~ 2, 
      
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ > 3 ~ 3, 
      
      # System 2
      (cntry == "NL" | cntry == "FR") &
        quota_educ > 0 & quota_educ < 4 ~ 1,       
      
      (cntry == "NL" | cntry == "FR") &
        quota_educ > 3 & quota_educ < 6 ~ 2,
      
      (cntry == "NL" | cntry == "FR") &
        quota_educ > 5 ~ 3,
      
      # System 3 
      cntry == "DE" &
        quota_educ > 0 & quota_educ < 3 ~ 1,       
      
      cntry == "DE" &
        quota_educ > 2 & quota_educ < 7 ~ 2, 
      
      cntry == "DE" &
        quota_educ > 6 ~ 3, 
      
      # CUNI
      cntry == "CZ" & 
        quota_educ > 0 &  quota_educ < 5 ~ 1, 
      cntry == "CZ" & 
        quota_educ > 4 &  quota_educ < 8 ~ 2,
      cntry == "CZ" & 
        quota_educ > 7 ~ 3,
      
      cntry == "ES" & 
        quota_educ > 0 &  quota_educ < 7 ~ 1, 
      cntry == "ES" & 
        quota_educ > 6 &  quota_educ < 10 ~ 2,
      cntry == "ES" & 
        quota_educ > 9 ~ 3,
      
      cntry == "SE" & 
        quota_educ == 1 ~ 1, 
      cntry == "SE" & 
        quota_educ > 1 &  quota_educ < 4 ~ 2,
      cntry == "SE" & 
        quota_educ > 3 ~ 3,
    ),
    educ_low = ifelse(quota_group_educ == 1, 1, 0), 
    educ_mid = ifelse(quota_group_educ == 2, 1, 0), 
    educ_high = ifelse(quota_group_educ == 3, 1, 0), 
    quota_group_educ = factor(quota_group_educ,levels=c(1,2,3))
)

table(w2$quota_group_educ)

# Create survey design objects for w1 and w2
w1_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = w1)
w2_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = w2)

table(w1$quota_group_educ)
table(w2$quota_educ_category)
table(w2$quota_group_educ)

```

```{r timing}

w1 %>% 
  group_by(cntry) %>% 
  summarise(
    median_time = median(duration)/60
  )

w2 %>% 
  group_by(cntry) %>% 
  summarise(
    median_time = median(duration)/60
  )

```

```{r general vars}

# Define colors for each policy
policy_colors <- c(
  "General mitigation" = "#9E3D22",                              # Dark orange
  "General adaptation" = "#2B5C8A",                              # Dodger blue
  "Tax fossil fuel profits" = "#EA782D",     # Light orange
  "Mandatory insulation" = "#FFD4A5",          # Medium orange
  "Building restrictions" = "#5386B3",        # Sky blue
  "Resilience investment" = "#C9E1F0" ,         # Steel blue
  #"Tax beef" = "#D2293D",
  "Tax flights" = "#D2293D" #FEB1A3
)

all_pols = c('EU_pol_build', 'EU_pol_waterconserve','gen_sup_mtgn','gen_sup_adpn', "CNTY_pol_insu", "CNTY_pol_taxff", "CNTY_pol_taxfly", "car_phase_all", "car_phase_exc", "ETS_gen_support", "ETS_II_transp", "ETS_II_heat", "ETS_II_ag", "CBAM", 
"EU_pol_beef", "EU_pol_rail", "EU_pol_pfli", "EU_pol_adban")# 'CNTY_pol_beef_w1','CNTY_pol_beef_w2', "CNTY_pol_beef",

w1_pols = c("CNTY_pol_insu", "CNTY_pol_taxff", "CNTY_pol_taxfly", "car_phase_all", "car_phase_exc", 
"ETS_gen_support", "ETS_II_transp", "ETS_II_heat", "ETS_II_ag", "CBAM", 
"EU_pol_beef", "EU_pol_rail", "EU_pol_pfli", "EU_pol_adban") #"CNTY_pol_beef",

w1_targ_pols = c("CNTY_pol_insu", "CNTY_pol_taxff","CNTY_pol_taxfly") # ,"CNTY_pol_beef"

w2_pols = c('EU_pol_build', 'EU_pol_waterconserve','gen_sup_mtgn','gen_sup_adpn')#"CNTY_pol_beef"

impact_labels <- c(
  "imp_exp_wb" = "Wellbeing",
  "imp_exp_econ" = "National\neconomy",
  "imp_exp_fin" = "Household\nfinances",
  "imp_exp_goal" = "Effectiveness",
  "fair_lowinc" = "On low income\nhouseholds*",
  "fair_mdlcls" = "On middle class\nhouseholds*"
)

preds_w1 <- c(
  "imp_exp_wb", "imp_exp_econ", "imp_exp_fin", "imp_exp_ghg_re",
  "gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", "poli_ori", "clim_worry", "val_bio1",
  "imp_exp_pol_assign", "cntry", "female", "quota_age",
  "quota_educ", "rural_urban"
)

preds_w2 <- c(
  "imp_exp_wb","imp_exp_econ", "imp_exp_fin", "imp_exp_CNTY_ghg", "imp_exp_EU_adap",
  "gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", "poli_ori", "clim_worry", "val_bio",
  "imp_exp_condition", "cntry", "female", "quota_age",
  "quota_educ", "rural_urban",'risk_falk_2','time_falk_2','inequality','ambiguity_0.10')


pols = c("CNTY_pol_insu", "CNTY_pol_taxff","CNTY_pol_taxfly",'EU_pol_build', 'EU_pol_waterconserve','gen_sup_mtgn','gen_sup_adpn')

```

```{r combine_data_long}
# Combine W1 and W2 data 


# Convert both to long for policy values, select/create common columns as necessary, then concat 
# Pivot longer while preserving the survey design
w1_long <- w1 %>%
  dplyr::select(cntry, weights, all_of(w1_targ_pols)) %>%
  mutate(mitig_adap = 0) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = w1_targ_pols,
               names_to = "policy",
               values_to = "score") %>%
  mutate(specific = 1)

w2_long <- w2 %>%
  dplyr::select(cntry, weights, all_of(w2_pols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = w2_pols,
               names_to = "policy",
               values_to = "score") %>% 
  mutate(
    specific = ifelse(policy %in% c('gen_sup_mtgn','gen_sup_adpn'), 0, 1),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(policy %in% c('gen_sup_adpn','EU_pol_build', 'EU_pol_waterconserve'), 1, 0)
  )

both <- rbind(w1_long, w2_long)

# Create survey design for the combined long data
both_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = both)

# write.csv(both,'w1_w2_combined_long.csv')
```

```{r beeftax}

w1 %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(CNTY_pol_beef, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(CNTY_pol_beef, na.rm = TRUE),
    count = n()
  )


w2 %>% 
  filter(cnty_percent == 10) %>% 
   summarise(
    mean = mean(CNTY_pol_beef, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(CNTY_pol_beef, na.rm = TRUE),
    count = n()
  )

w2 %>% 
  filter(cnty_percent == 50) %>% 
   summarise(
    mean = mean(CNTY_pol_beef, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(CNTY_pol_beef, na.rm = TRUE),
    count = n()
  )

summary(lm(CNTY_pol_beef ~ as.factor(cnty_percent),data=w2))


w1_long_beef <- w1_full %>%
  dplyr::select(cntry, weights, all_of('CNTY_pol_beef')) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = 'CNTY_pol_beef',
               names_to = "policy",
               values_to = "score") 

# Calculate weighted proportion supporting each policy (score >= 5)
support_stats <- w1_long_beef %>%
  mutate(supports = ifelse(score >= 5, 1, 0)) %>%
  svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = .) %>%
  svyby(
    ~supports,
    ~policy + cntry,  # Added cntry here
    design = .,
    FUN = svymean,
    na.rm = TRUE,
    vartype = c("se", "ci")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_support = supports * 100,
    pct_se = se * 100,
    pct_lower = ci_l * 100,
    pct_upper = ci_u * 100
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(policy, cntry, pct_support, pct_se, pct_lower, pct_upper)  # Added cntry here too

print(support_stats)

```

```{r combine_data_normal}

w1_rec <- w1 %>%   
  mutate(across(
    c(CNTY_pol_taxff, CNTY_pol_insu, CNTY_pol_taxfly),
    as.numeric
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    imp_exp_ghg_re     = 8 - imp_exp_ghg, # first, recode the ghg emissions red socres
    mitig_adap = 0,
    imp_exp_goal = imp_exp_ghg_re, # set it to goal expectancy 
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_pol_assign),
    pol_all = case_when(
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 2 ~ CNTY_pol_taxff,
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 3 ~ CNTY_pol_insu,
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 4 ~ CNTY_pol_taxfly,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_real_
    ),
    cntry = factor(cntry),
    female             = factor(female),
    quota_educ = factor(quota_educ),
    quota_group_educ = factor(quota_group_educ), 
    quota_age          = factor(quota_age),
    rural_urban        = factor(rural_urban),
    rural = factor(ifelse(rural_urban == 1 | rural_urban == 2, 1, 0)),
    imp_exp_pol_assign = case_when(
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 2  ~ 'Tax fossil fuel profits',
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 3 ~ 'Mandatory insulation',
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 4 ~ 'Tax flights',
      TRUE                   ~ NA),
    EU_pol_build = NA,
    EU_pol_waterconserve = NA,
    gen_sup_mtgn = NA,
    gen_sup_adpn = NA,
    fair_lowinc = NA,
    fair_mdlcls = NA
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
     c(imp_exp_pol_assign, imp_exp_goal, pol_all, cntry, val_bio1, female,rural,
      quota_age, quota_educ, rural_urban, mitig_adap, clim_worry, gen_trust_cnty,quota_group_educ,
      gen_trust_EU, poli_ori,imp_exp_wb,imp_exp_econ,imp_exp_fin,hh_inc,fair_lowinc,fair_mdlcls), all_of(w1_targ_pols), all_of(w2_pols), weights, wave
  )


w2_rec <- w2 %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(w2_pols),
    as.numeric
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    imp_exp_ghg_re = 8 - imp_exp_CNTY_ghg,
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_condition),
    imp_exp_goal = case_when(
      (imp_exp_pol_assign == 'build' | imp_exp_pol_assign == 'waterconserve') ~ imp_exp_EU_adap,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_real_
    ),
    pol_all = case_when(
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 'build' ~ EU_pol_build,
      imp_exp_pol_assign == 'waterconserve' ~ EU_pol_waterconserve,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_real_
    ),
     imp_exp_pol_assign = case_when(
      imp_exp_condition == 'build' ~ 'Building restrictions',
      imp_exp_condition == 'waterconserve' ~ 'Resilience investment',
      TRUE                   ~ NA
    ),
    cntry = factor(cntry),
    val_bio1 = val_bio,
    female = ifelse(quota_gndr == 2, 1, 0), 
    female             = factor(female),
    quota_educ = factor(quota_educ),
    quota_group_educ = factor(quota_group_educ),
    quota_age          = factor(quota_age),
    rural_urban        = factor(rural_urban),
    rural = factor(ifelse(rural_urban == 1 | rural_urban == 2, 1, 0)),
    mitig_adap = case_when(
      (imp_exp_pol_assign == 'Building restrictions' | imp_exp_pol_assign == 'Resilience investment') ~ 1,
    ),
    CNTY_pol_insu = NA,
    CNTY_pol_taxff = NA,
    CNTY_pol_taxfly = NA,
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    c(imp_exp_pol_assign, imp_exp_goal, pol_all, cntry, val_bio1, female, rural,
      quota_age, quota_educ,quota_group_educ, rural_urban, mitig_adap, clim_worry, gen_trust_cnty,
      gen_trust_EU, poli_ori,imp_exp_wb,imp_exp_econ,imp_exp_fin,hh_inc,fair_lowinc,fair_mdlcls), all_of(w2_pols),all_of(w1_targ_pols), weights, wave
  ) 



# Combine 
full_rec = rbind(w1_rec,w2_rec)
table(full_rec$rural)
table(full_rec$quota_group_educ)

# Fix education -- not necessary
full_rec <- full_rec %>% 
  mutate(
    quota_group_educ = case_when(
      
      # Prefer not to answer to NA
      quota_educ < 0 ~ NA, 
      
      # System 1
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ == 1 ~ 1, 
      
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ == 2 ~ 2, 
      
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ == 3 ~ 2, 
      
      (cntry == "AT" | cntry == "DK" | cntry == "GR" | cntry == "HU" | cntry == "IT" | cntry == "PL" | cntry == "SI") &
        quota_educ > 3 ~ 3, 
      
      # System 2
      (cntry == "NL" | cntry == "FR") &
        quota_educ > 0 & quota_educ < 4 ~ 1,       
      
      (cntry == "NL" | cntry == "FR") &
        quota_educ > 3 & quota_educ < 6 ~ 2,
      
      (cntry == "NL" | cntry == "FR") &
        quota_educ > 5 ~ 3,
      
      # System 3 
      cntry == "DE" &
        quota_educ > 0 & quota_educ < 3 ~ 1,       
      
      cntry == "DE" &
        quota_educ > 2 & quota_educ < 7 ~ 2, 
      
      cntry == "DE" &
        quota_educ > 6 ~ 3, 
      
      # CUNI
      cntry == "CZ" & 
        quota_educ > 0 &  quota_educ < 5 ~ 1, 
      cntry == "CZ" & 
        quota_educ > 4 &  quota_educ < 8 ~ 2,
      cntry == "CZ" & 
        quota_educ > 7 ~ 3,
      
      cntry == "ES" & 
        quota_educ > 0 &  quota_educ < 7 ~ 1, 
      cntry == "ES" & 
        quota_educ > 6 &  quota_educ < 10 ~ 2,
      cntry == "ES" & 
        quota_educ > 9 ~ 3,
      
      cntry == "SE" & 
        quota_educ == 1 ~ 1, 
      cntry == "SE" & 
        quota_educ > 1 &  quota_educ < 4 ~ 2,
      cntry == "SE" & 
        quota_educ > 3 ~ 3,
    ),
    educ_low = ifelse(quota_group_educ == 1, 1, 0), 
    educ_mid = ifelse(quota_group_educ == 2, 1, 0), 
    educ_high = ifelse(quota_group_educ == 3, 1, 0), 
    quota_group_educ = factor(quota_group_educ,levels=c(1,2,3)),#,labels=c('Low','Mid','High'))
  )

# Check female variable
full_rec %>% 
  group_by(female) %>% 
  summarise(across(pols, ~ mean(., na.rm = TRUE)))

# Check age variable
full_rec %>% 
  group_by(quota_age) %>% 
  summarise(across(pols, ~ mean(., na.rm = TRUE)))

#write.csv(full_rec,'w1_w2_combined.csv')

```

```{r standardise}

# Standardise required variables within each wave

standardize_continuous <- function(data, vars_to_standardize, weights_var = "weights") {
  data_std <- data
  
  for (var in vars_to_standardize) {
    if (is.numeric(data[[var]])) {
      # Weighted mean and SD
      w_mean <- weighted.mean(data[[var]], w = data[[weights_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
      w_var <- weighted.mean((data[[var]] - w_mean)^2, 
                             w = data[[weights_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
      w_sd <- sqrt(w_var)
      
      # Standardize
      data_std[[paste0(var, "_z")]] <- (data[[var]] - w_mean) / w_sd
    }
  }
  
  return(data_std)
}

# Apply to each wave based on recoded version of the wave data
wave1_std <- standardize_continuous(w1_rec, 
                                    vars_to_standardize = c("clim_worry", "val_bio1",
                                                            'poli_ori','gen_trust_cnty',
                                                            'gen_trust_EU','hh_inc',
                                                            'imp_exp_econ','imp_exp_goal',
                                                            'imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_fin'),
                                    weights_var = "weights")

wave2_std <- standardize_continuous(w2_rec, 
                                    vars_to_standardize = c("clim_worry", "val_bio1",
                                                            'poli_ori','gen_trust_cnty',
                                                            'gen_trust_EU','hh_inc',
                                                            'imp_exp_econ','imp_exp_goal',
                                                            'imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_fin'),
                                    weights_var = "weights")
class(wave2_std$quota_group_educ)

# Combine standardised wave data 
full_rec_std = rbind(wave1_std, wave2_std)
```

```{r balance}
library(estimatr)

# table(full_rec$quota_age)
# full_rec_sub <- full_rec %>%
#   mutate(
#     quota_educ = factor(quota_educ),
#     #quota_age = factor(quota_age)
#   ) %>% 
#   select(c(wave,quota_educ,quota_age))
# 
# table(full_rec_sub$wave)
# 
# balance_table(full_rec_sub,'wave')
# 
# datasummary_skim(data_sub,type='categorical')
# datasummary_balance(~wave, data = full_rec_sub,dinm = T,dinm_statistic = 'p.value')
# names(data)

table(w1$quota_educ)
table(w2$quota_educ)

table(w1$quota_group_educ)
table(w1$quota_group_educ)

table(w1_rec$rural)/nrow(w1)*100
table(w2_rec$rural)/nrow(w2)*100

# Create a survey design object with your weights
cntry_targ = 'DE'

svy_design <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,  # No clustering
  weights = ~weights,
  data = full_rec #%>% filter(cntry == cntry_targ)
)
#svychisq(~wave + quota_educ, design = svy_design)
# svychisq(~ wave + rural, design = svy_design)

sub = full_rec %>% filter(cntry == cntry_targ)
wave = 2
table(sub[sub$wave == wave,]$rural)/nrow(sub[sub$wave == wave,])*100
svychisq(~ wave + rural, design = svy_design)

# For factor variables - Weighted chi-square tests
svychisq(~wave + quota_age, design = svy_design)
svychisq(~wave + female, design = svy_design)
svychisq(~wave + quota_group_educ, design = svy_design)

# For numeric variables - Weighted t-tests
svyttest(hh_inc ~ wave, design = svy_design)
svyttest(clim_worry ~ wave, design = svy_design)
svyttest(val_bio1 ~ wave, design = svy_design)
svyttest(poli_ori ~ wave, design = svy_design)
svyttest(gen_trust_cnty ~ wave, design = svy_design)
svyttest(gen_trust_EU ~ wave, design = svy_design)
table(full_rec[full_rec$wave == 1,]$cntry)
table(full_rec[full_rec$wave == 2,]$cntry)

sub = full_rec %>% filter(cntry == 'NL')
wave = 2
table(sub[sub$wave == wave,]$quota_group_educ)/nrow(sub[sub$wave == wave,])*100

# For weighted summary statistics by wave
svyby(~clim_worry + poli_ori + hh_inc + val_bio1 + gen_trust_EU + gen_trust_cnty, ~wave, design = svy_design, svymean, na.rm = TRUE)

# For weighted proportions of factor variables by wave
svyby(~quota_age, ~wave, design = svy_design, svymean)
svyby(~female, ~wave, design = svy_design, svymean)
svyby(~quota_educ, ~wave, design = svy_design, svymean)
svyby(~quota_group_educ, ~wave, design = svy_design, svymean)
svyby(~rural, ~wave, design = svy_design, svymean)

median(w2$clim_worry)

```

```{r overall_stats}

library(survey)

# Create survey design for the 'both' dataset
both_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = both)

# Calculate weighted proportion supporting each policy (score >= 5)
support_stats <- both %>%
  mutate(supports = ifelse(score >= 5, 1, 0)) %>%
  svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = .) %>%
  svyby(
    ~supports,
    ~policy,
    design = .,
    FUN = svymean,
    na.rm = TRUE,
    vartype = c("se", "ci")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_support = supports * 100,
    pct_se = se * 100,
    pct_lower = ci_l * 100,
    pct_upper = ci_u * 100
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(policy, pct_support, pct_se, pct_lower, pct_upper)

print(support_stats)

```

theme standard
```{r}
theme_standard <- function(base_size = 11) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = base_size * 1.2, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_size),
      plot.caption  = element_text(size = base_size * 1),
      
      axis.title    = element_text(size = base_size * 1.15),
      axis.text     = element_text(size = base_size * 0.9),
      
      legend.title  = element_text(size = base_size),
      legend.text   = element_text(size = base_size * 1),
      strip.text    = element_text(size = base_size * 1.15, face = "bold"),
      strip.text.x = element_text(size = base_size * 1.15, face = "bold"),
strip.text.y = element_text(size = base_size * 1.15, face = "bold")
    )
}
```




```{r Fig1A}

policies_cols = c('gen_sup_mtgn','gen_sup_adpn')

# Filter and prepare data
policies_long_raw <- both %>%
  filter(policy %in% policies_cols) %>%
  mutate(
    policy = factor(policy),
    policy = fct_recode(policy,
                        "General mitigation" = "gen_sup_mtgn",
                        "General adaptation" = "gen_sup_adpn")
  )

policies_long_raw %>% group_by(mitig_adap) %>% summarise(mean = mean(score))

# Create survey design for filtered data
policies_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = policies_long_raw)

# Calculate WEIGHTED means for ordering
policy_means <- svyby(~score, ~policy, design = policies_design, FUN = svymean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Reorder factor levels by weighted mean
policies_long_raw <- policies_long_raw %>%
  left_join(policy_means %>% dplyr::select(policy, score), by = "policy") %>%
  rename(weighted_mean = score.y, score = score.x) %>%
  mutate(policy = fct_reorder(policy, weighted_mean,na.rm=T))

library(ggridges)

fig1A <- ggplot(
  policies_long_raw,
  aes(x = policy, y = score, fill = policy)
) +
  geom_violin(
    trim = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.35,
    width = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.2,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "General mitigation" = "#9E3D22",
    "General adaptation" = "#2B5C8A"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 7.5), breaks = 1:7) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Support (1–7)",
    fill = "Policy type"
  ) +
  theme_standard() +
  coord_flip()

fig1A

#Alternative Alessio

# keep only valid Likert values
df_ok <- policies_long_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(score), score >= 1, score <= 7)

# proportions for the 7 bars per policy
dist_df <- df_ok %>%
  filter(!is.na(weights), weights >= 0) %>%          # keep valid weights
  group_by(policy, score) %>%
  summarise(w = sum(weights), .groups = "drop") %>%  # weighted count per Likert point
  group_by(policy) %>%
  mutate(prop = w / sum(w)) %>%                      # weighted share
  ungroup()

# box components per policy (quartiles + whiskers)
library(dplyr)
library(Hmisc)

box_df <- df_ok %>%
  filter(!is.na(weights), weights >= 0) %>%
  group_by(policy) %>%
  summarise(
    q1  = as.numeric(wtd.quantile(score, weights, probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)),
    med = as.numeric(wtd.quantile(score, weights, probs = 0.50, na.rm = TRUE)),
    q3  = as.numeric(wtd.quantile(score, weights, probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)),
    lo  = min(score, na.rm = TRUE),   # whiskers: keep unweighted (standard practice)
    hi  = max(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# choose where the box sits on the y-axis (same in both panels)
ymin_box <- 0.1
ymax_box <- 0.2

fig1A <- ggplot() +
  # grey histogram bars (proportions)
  geom_col(
    data = dist_df,
    aes(x = score, y = prop),
    fill = "grey75",
    alpha = 0.5,
    width = 0.9
  ) +
  # IQR box
  geom_rect(
    data = box_df,
    aes(xmin = q1, xmax = q3, ymin = ymin_box, ymax = ymax_box, fill = policy),
    alpha = 0.85,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  # median line inside the box
  geom_segment(
    data = box_df,
    aes(x = med, xend = med, y = ymin_box, yend = ymax_box),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  # whiskers (optional but nice)
  geom_segment(
    data = box_df,
    aes(x = lo, xend = q1, y = (ymin_box + ymax_box)/2, yend = (ymin_box + ymax_box)/2),
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = box_df,
    aes(x = q3, xend = hi, y = (ymin_box + ymax_box)/2, yend = (ymin_box + ymax_box)/2),
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ policy, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "General mitigation" = "#9E3D22",
    "General adaptation" = "#2B5C8A"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:7, limits = c(0.5, 7.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.35)) +
  labs(x = "Support (1–7)", y = "Proportion", fill = "Policy type") +
  theme_standard() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

mean_df <- df_ok %>%
  group_by(policy) %>%
  summarise(
    wmean = weighted.mean(score, w = weights, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(y = (ymin_box + ymax_box) / 2)

tfig1A <- fig1A +
  geom_point(
    data = mean_df,
    aes(x = wmean, y = y),
    color = "red",
    size = 3,
    shape = 18,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  )

fig1A

ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig1A_box_WEIGHTED_Alessio.png')
```

```{r Fig1B}

trdata <- w2[w2$trdoff_mt_ad_updatedvalues_check=='Did Update',]
#trdata$trdoff_mt

# FOR THE COUNTRY LEVEL

# Create survey design
trdata_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = trdata)

# Calculate weighted country statistics
country_stats <- svyby(
  ~trdoff_mt, 
  ~cntry, 
  design = trdata_design, 
  FUN = svymean, 
  na.rm = TRUE,
  vartype = c("se", "ci")
)

# Clean up the column names
country_stats <- country_stats %>%
  rename(
    mean_pref = trdoff_mt,
    se_pref = se,
    lower_ci = ci_l,
    upper_ci = ci_u
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_pref)

# Calculate weighted total mean
total_mean <- svymean(~trdoff_mt, design = trdata_design, na.rm = TRUE)[1]

# Create the plot
ggplot(country_stats, aes(x = reorder(cntry, mean_pref), y = mean_pref, color = cntry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = total_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Preference for Adaptation vs Mitigation by Country",
    subtitle = "0 = Complete Adaptation Preference, 100 = Complete Mitigation Preference",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Mean Preference (0–100)",
    color = "Country"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()


# Overall distribution
fig1B <- ggplot(trdata, aes(x = trdoff_mt, weight = weights)) +
  #geom_histogram(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5, bins = 40) +  # adjust bins as needed
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(count/sum(count))), 
                 fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5, bins = 40) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Preference (0 = Adaptation, 100 = Mitigation)",
    y = "Weighted frequency"
  ) +
  theme_standard()+
  theme(strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ))
fig1B
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig1B_hist_WEIGHTED_props.png')

# Combine Figures 1A and 1B
fig1AB <- ggarrange(fig1A,fig1B,labels = c('A','B'),nrow=2)
fig1AB
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig1AB_box_hist_WEIGHTED_alessio.png')

# Distribution by country for appendix
cntry_trade <- ggplot(trdata, aes(x = trdoff_mt, weight = weights)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5,bins=40) +
  facet_wrap(~ cntry, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    #title = "Distribution of Mitigation vs Adaptation",
    x = "Preference (0 = Adaptation, 100 = Mitigation)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()
cntry_trade

ggsave('figures_r1/final/CountryTradeOff_WEIGHTED.png')

#ggarrange(cntry_supp,cntry_trade)

```

```{r Fig2A}

# Create summary statistics 
library(survey)

# Create survey design for the combined data
both_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = both)

# Calculate weighted summary statistics
summary_data <- svyby(
  ~score, 
  ~cntry + policy + specific + mitig_adap, 
  design = both_design, 
  FUN = svymean, 
  na.rm = TRUE,
  vartype = c("se", "ci")
) %>%
  rename(
    mean_score = score,
    se = se,
    lower_ci = ci_l,
    upper_ci = ci_u
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    policy = fct_recode(policy,
                        "General mitigation" = "gen_sup_mtgn",
                        "General adaptation" = "gen_sup_adpn",
                        "Resilience investment" = "EU_pol_waterconserve",
                        "Building restrictions" = "EU_pol_build",
                        "Tax fossil fuel profits" = "CNTY_pol_taxff",
                        "Mandatory insulation" = "CNTY_pol_insu",
                        "Tax flights" = 'CNTY_pol_taxfly'
    ),
    specific = factor(specific, levels = c(0, 1), 
                     labels = c('General policy', 'Specific policy')),
    policy_type = interaction(specific, mitig_adap, sep = "_")
  )

# Create color mapping
summary_data <- summary_data %>%
  mutate(
    color_group = case_when(
      policy == "General mitigation" ~ "General mitigation",
      policy == "General adaptation" ~ "General adaptation",
      policy %in% c("Tax fossil fuel profits", "Mandatory insulation",'Tax flights') ~ 
        paste0("", policy),
      policy %in% c("Resilience investment", "Building restrictions") ~ 
        paste0("", policy)
    ),
    policy = factor(policy, levels = c('General mitigation','General adaptation',
                                        'Tax on fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions')),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap, levels = c(0,1), labels = c('Mitigation','Adaptation'))
  )

# Rest of your plotting code stays the same
cntry_supp <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = cntry, y = mean_score, 
                                        color = color_group)) +
  geom_point(data = summary_data %>% filter(specific == "Specific policy"),
             aes(shape = mitig_adap),
             size = 4) +
  geom_point(data = summary_data %>% filter(specific == "General policy"),
             aes(shape = mitig_adap),
             size = 6) +
  geom_errorbar(data = summary_data %>% filter(specific == "General policy"),
                aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci),
                width = 0.1) +
  geom_errorbar(data = summary_data %>% filter(specific == "Specific policy"),
                aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci),
                width = 0.1) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = rev) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=4, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 7), breaks = seq(1,7,1),
                     labels = c('1: Strongly\nopposed','2','3','4: Neutral',
                                '5','6','7: Strongly\nin favour')) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),
                     name = "Policy Type") +
  scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c('General mitigation','General adaptation',
                                'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                'Mandatory insulation',
                                'Tax flights',
                                'Resilience investment',
                                'Building restrictions')) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Average Support"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size=11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x.bottom = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.y.left = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size=11)
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      nrow = 2,
      override.aes = list(
        shape = c(17, 16, 17, 17, 17, 16, 16)
      )
    ),
    shape = guide_legend(nrow = 2)
  )

cntry_supp

cntry_supp <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = cntry, y = mean_score, color = color_group)) +
  geom_point(
    data = summary_data %>% dplyr::filter(specific == "Specific policy"),
    aes(shape = mitig_adap),
    size = 4
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = summary_data %>% dplyr::filter(specific == "General policy"),
    aes(shape = mitig_adap),
    size = 6
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = summary_data %>% dplyr::filter(specific == "General policy"),
    aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci),
    width = 0.1
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = summary_data %>% dplyr::filter(specific == "Specific policy"),
    aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci),
    width = 0.1
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(1, 7), breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c("1: Strongly\nopposed","2","3","4: Neutral","5","6","7: Strongly\nin favour")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = rev) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16), name = "Policy Type") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = policy_colors,
    name = "Policy",
    breaks = c(
      "General mitigation","General adaptation",
      "Tax fossil fuel profits","Mandatory insulation","Tax flights",
      "Resilience investment","Building restrictions"
    )
  ) +
  labs(title = "", x = "Country", y = "Average Support") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),

    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x.bottom = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.y.left = element_blank(),

    # if you ever facet this plot later, these match your imp_p style:
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    )
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      nrow = 1,
      override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 16, 17, 17, 17, 16, 16))
    ),
    shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  )
cntry_supp

ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig2A_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',width=14,height=10)
  
```

```{r Fig2B}

# Filter and prepare data with weights
impact_long <- full_rec %>%
  filter(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Building restrictions','Resilience investment',
                                    'Mandatory insulation','Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                    "Tax flights")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(imp_exp_wb, imp_exp_econ, imp_exp_fin, imp_exp_goal),
    names_to = "impact_category",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(score))  # Remove missing values

# Create survey design
impact_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = impact_long)

policy_levels <- c("Mandatory insulation","Tax fossil fuel profits",
                    "Resilience investment","Building restrictions",
                    "Tax flights")

# Calculate weighted summary statistics
impact_by_policy <- svyby(
  ~score, 
  ~imp_exp_pol_assign + impact_category, 
  design = impact_design, 
  FUN = svymean, 
  na.rm = TRUE,
  vartype = c("se", "ci")
) %>%
  rename(
    mean = score,
    se = se,
    ci_lower = ci_l,
    ci_upper = ci_u
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_pol_assign, levels = policy_levels),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c("Resilience investment","Building restrictions"), 1, 0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap, levels = c(0,1), labels = c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    impact_category = factor(impact_category, levels = rev(c('imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_goal',
                                                              'imp_exp_fin','imp_exp_econ',
                                                              'fair_lowinc','fair_mdlcls'))),
    factor_group = ifelse(impact_category %in% c('imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_goal'), 1, 0),
    factor_group = factor(factor_group, levels = c(1,0), labels = c('Benefits','Costs'))
  )

# Rest of your plotting code stays the same
imp_p <- ggplot(impact_by_policy, aes(x = impact_category, y = mean, 
                                       color = imp_exp_pol_assign,
                                       shape = mitig_adap, group = factor_group)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.05) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = impact_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(1, 7), breaks = 1:7,
                     labels = c('1: Very\nnegatively','2','3','4: No impact',
                               '5','6','7: Very\npositively')) +
  scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c('Tax fossil fuel profits',
                               'Mandatory insulation',
                               'Tax flights',
                               'Resilience investment',
                               'Building restrictions')) +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(factor_group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space = "free_y",
    switch = "y",
    labeller = labeller(group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "",
    y = "Average Impact Perception",
    color = "Policy"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),
                     name = "Policy Type") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size=11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size=11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=11),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
  angle = 90,
  hjust = 0.5,
  vjust = 0.5,
  size = 12,
  face = "bold",
  margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"),
   strip.background.y = element_rect(
  fill = NA,
  colour = "grey75",
  linewidth = 0.4
)
  ) +
  guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17, 16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4, linetype = 'dashed', color = 'grey40') +
  coord_flip()

imp_p
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig2B_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',width=12,height=6)



ggpubr::ggarrange(
  cntry_supp,
  imp_p,
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  nrow = 2,        # <- FORCE rows
  ncol = 1,
  common.legend = TRUE,
  legend = "bottom"
)

ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig2AB_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',width=14,height=8)

```

```{r sim_coeffs_prep_packages}

# Define which packages needed for analyses
p_needed <-
  c("knitr",
    "dplyr", 
    "stargazer",
    "ggplot2",
    "viridis",
    "haven",
    "MASS",
    "modelsummary",
    "tidyr",
    "data.table",
    "scales",
    'arm')

# Check which packages are already installed on your computer
packages <- rownames(installed.packages())

# Check which packages are not installed
p_to_install <- p_needed[!(p_needed %in% packages)]

if (length(p_to_install) > 0) {
  install.packages(p_to_install)
}
sapply(p_needed, require, character.only = TRUE)

```

```{r sim_function}
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulation function
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

simulate_expected_change_std <- function(varname, model, sims, data, weights_var = "weights") {
  
  # Build baseline
  base <- data %>% 
    summarise(across(
      -weights_var, 
      ~ {
        if (is.numeric(.)) {
          weighted.mean(., w = data[[weights_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
        } else if (is.factor(.) || is.character(.)) {
          tbl <- tapply(data[[weights_var]], ., sum, na.rm = TRUE)
          modal_value <- names(which.max(tbl))
          if (is.factor(.)) {
            factor(modal_value, levels = levels(.))
          } else {
            modal_value
          }
        } else {
          mean(., na.rm = TRUE)
        }
      }
    )) 
  
  # Determine min/max values
  if (is.factor(data[[varname]]) || is.character(data[[varname]])) {
    var_levels <- levels(factor(data[[varname]]))
    if (length(var_levels) < 2) return(NULL)
    
    min_val <- var_levels[1]
    max_val <- var_levels[length(var_levels)]
    
  } else {
    # For standardized variables, use -1 SD to +1 SD (or quartiles if you prefer)
    # Option A: -1 SD to +1 SD
    min_val <- -1
    max_val <- 1
    
    # Option B: Still use weighted quartiles (works for standardized too)
    # min_val <- weightedQuantile(data[[varname]], w = data[[weights_var]], 
    #                             probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
    # max_val <- weightedQuantile(data[[varname]], w = data[[weights_var]], 
    #                             probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Create min and max scenarios
  x_min <- base
  x_max <- base
  x_min[[varname]] <- min_val
  x_max[[varname]] <- max_val
  
  # Ensure factor levels match
  for (col in names(base)) {
    if (is.factor(data[[col]])) {
      x_min[[col]] <- factor(x_min[[col]], levels = levels(data[[col]]))
      x_max[[col]] <- factor(x_max[[col]], levels = levels(data[[col]]))
    }
  }
  
  # Predictions
  tryCatch({
    X_min <- model.matrix(delete.response(terms(model)), data = x_min)
    X_max <- model.matrix(delete.response(terms(model)), data = x_max)
    
    expected_min <- X_min %*% t(sims$coef)
    expected_max <- X_max %*% t(sims$coef)
    
    diffs <- as.vector(expected_max - expected_min)
    
    tibble(
      variable = varname,
      mean_diff = mean(diffs),
      lower_95 = quantile(diffs, 0.025),
      upper_95 = quantile(diffs, 0.975)
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    warning(paste("Error processing", varname, ":", e$message))
    return(NULL)
  })
}


```

```{r sim_run}

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Subset the data (mitig vs adap, all rel preds)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# preds_overall = c("gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", 
#           "poli_ori", 
#           "clim_worry",
#           "val_bio1",
#           "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc',"quota_group_educ"
#           ) 
# 
# 
# 
# preds = c("gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", 
#           "poli_ori", 
#           "clim_worry",
#           "val_bio1",
#           "imp_exp_wb","imp_exp_goal","imp_exp_econ","imp_exp_fin",
#           "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc',"quota_group_educ"
#           ) 

preds_overall_z = c("gen_trust_cnty_z", "gen_trust_EU_z", 
          "poli_ori_z", 
          "clim_worry_z",
          "val_bio1_z",
          "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc_z',"quota_group_educ"
          ) 

preds_z = c("gen_trust_cnty_z", "gen_trust_EU_z", 
          "poli_ori_z", 
          "clim_worry_z",
          "val_bio1_z",
          "imp_exp_wb_z","imp_exp_goal_z","imp_exp_econ_z","imp_exp_fin_z",
          "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc_z',"quota_group_educ"
          ) 

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit the models - BE SURE TO USE STANDARDISED DATA AND VARIABLES
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## gen mitig
sub = wave2_std
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_genmtgn <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_overall_z, response = "gen_sup_mtgn"),
                    design = survey_design,
                    family = gaussian(),  
             data = sub)

car::vif(m_genmtgn)
summary(m_genmtgn)

## gen adaptation
m_genadpn <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_overall_z, response = "gen_sup_adpn"),
                   design = survey_design,
                    family = gaussian(),  #
             data = sub)

car::vif(m_genadpn)

## CNTY_pol_insu
sub = wave1_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_insu <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_z, response = "CNTY_pol_insu"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  
             data = sub
             )  
car::vif(m_insu)
summary(m_insu)

## CNTY_pol_taxff
sub = wave1_std  %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_taxff <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_z, response = "CNTY_pol_taxff"),
                  design = survey_design,
                  family = gaussian(), 
                  data = sub
             )
## CNTY_pol_taxfly
sub = wave1_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_taxfly <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_z, response = "CNTY_pol_taxfly"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(), 
             data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_waterconserve
sub = wave2_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_waterconserve <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_z, response = "EU_pol_waterconserve"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(), 
             data = sub
             )
car::vif(m_waterconserve)

## EU_pol_build
sub = wave2_std   %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_build <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_z, response = "EU_pol_build"),
            design = survey_design,
            family = gaussian(),  
            data = sub
             )
car::vif(m_build)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulate coefficients (one for each model)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract simulations from the weighted model
library(arm) 
set.seed(123)

# Function to simulate coefficients from svyglm
sim_svyglm <- function(model, n.sims = 1000) {
  # Extract coefficients and variance-covariance matrix
  beta_hat <- coef(model)
  V <- vcov(model)
  
  # Simulate from multivariate normal
  library(MASS)
  beta_sims <- mvrnorm(n.sims, mu = beta_hat, Sigma = V)
  
  # Return in a format similar to sim() output
  list(
    coef = beta_sims,
    sigma = summary(model)$dispersion  # For reference
  )
}

## genmtgn
sims_genmtgn <- sim_svyglm(m_genmtgn, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_genmtgn <- coef(sims_genmtgn)  # matrix: rows = sims, columns = coefficients

## genadpn
sims_genadpn <- sim_svyglm(m_genadpn, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_genadpn <- coef(sims_genadpn) 

## insu
sims_insu <- sim_svyglm(m_insu, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_insu <- coef(sims_insu) 

## taxff
sims_taxff <- sim_svyglm(m_taxff, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_taxff <- coef(sims_taxff) 

## taxfly
sims_taxfly <- sim_svyglm(m_taxfly, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_taxfly <- coef(sims_taxfly) 

## waterconserve
sims_waterconserve <- sim_svyglm(m_waterconserve, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_waterconserve <- coef(sims_waterconserve) 

## build
sims_build <- sim_svyglm(m_build, n.sims = 1000)
#betas_build <- coef(sims_build) 

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Apply
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# For troubleshooting 
# for (var in preds_overall) {
#   cat("Testing:", var, "\n")
#   print(table(full_rec[[var]], useNA = "ifany"))
#   tryCatch({
#     result <- simulate_expected_change_unst(var, m_genmtgn, sims_genmtgn, full_rec, weights_var = "weights")
#     print(result)
#   }, error = function(e) {
#     cat("ERROR:", e$message, "\n\n")
#   })
# }


# genmtgn
EV_genmtgn <- map_dfr(preds_overall_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_genmtgn, sims_genmtgn, wave2_std %>% dplyr::select(all_of(preds_overall_z),weights,gen_sup_mtgn)),weights_var = 'weights')

# genadpn
EV_genadpn <- map_dfr(preds_overall_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_genadpn, sims_genadpn, wave2_std %>% dplyr::select(all_of(preds_overall_z),weights,gen_sup_adpn)),weights_var = 'weights')

# insu
EV_insu <- map_dfr(preds_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_insu, sims_insu, wave1_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation")),weights_var='weights')

# taxff
EV_taxff <- map_dfr(preds_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_taxff, sims_taxff, wave1_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits")),weights_var='weights')

# taxfly
EV_taxfly <- map_dfr(preds_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_taxfly, sims_taxfly, wave1_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")),weights_var='weights')

# waterconserve
EV_waterconserve <- map_dfr(preds_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_waterconserve, sims_waterconserve, wave2_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")),weights_var='weights')

# build 
EV_build <- map_dfr(preds_z, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_build, sims_build, wave2_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")),weights_var='weights')


## combine
EV_genmtgn    <- EV_genmtgn    %>% mutate(policy = "General mitigation")
EV_genadpn   <- EV_genadpn   %>% mutate(policy = "General adaptation")
EV_insu <- EV_insu %>% mutate(policy = "Mandatory insulation")
EV_taxff <- EV_taxff %>% mutate(policy = "Tax fossil fuel profits")
EV_taxfly <- EV_taxfly %>% mutate(policy = "Tax flights")
EV_waterconserve <- EV_waterconserve %>% mutate(policy = "Resilience investment")
EV_build <- EV_build %>% mutate(policy = "Building restrictions")

```

```{r fIG1C_FIG3}
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prep plot
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
term_levels <- c(
  "Trust, country","Trust, EU",
  "Political orientation", 
  "Climate worry",# 
  "Biospheric value",
   "Well-being","Effectiveness","National\neconomy","Household\nfinances",
  "Female","Age","Education","Rural","Household\nIncome"
)

group_levels <- c("Socio-demographics",
                 'Policy Evaluations',
                  "Attitudes"
                  )

policy_levels <- c("General mitigation", "General adaptation",
                   "Mandatory insulation","Tax fossil fuel profits",
                   "Tax flights",
                    "Resilience investment","Building restrictions")#

## FOR PLOTTING THE OVERALL OBJECTIVES 
all_effects_overall <- bind_rows(EV_genmtgn, EV_genadpn)

all_effects_overall <- all_effects_overall %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = case_match(
      variable,
      "gen_trust_cnty_z" ~ "Trust, country",
      "gen_trust_EU_z" ~ "Trust, EU",
      "poli_ori_z" ~ "Political orientation",
      "clim_worry_z" ~ "Climate worry",
      "val_bio1_z" ~ "Biospheric value",
      "female" ~ "Female",
      "quota_age" ~ "Age",
      "quota_group_educ" ~ "Education",
      "rural" ~ "Rural",
      "hh_inc_z" ~ "Household Income",
      .default = variable
    ),
    group = case_match(
      variable,
      "gen_trust_cnty_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "gen_trust_EU_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "poli_ori_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "clim_worry_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "val_bio1_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "female" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "quota_age" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "quota_group_educ" ~ "Socio-demographics",
       "rural" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "hh_inc_z" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      .default = variable
    ),
    term_label = factor(term_label, levels = term_levels),
    group = factor(group, levels = rev(group_levels)),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(policy %in% c('Building restrictions','Resilience investment','General adaptation'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap,levels=c(0,1),labels=c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    policy = factor(policy, levels = policy_levels),
    policy_plot = factor(policy, levels = rev(policy_levels))
  )

## FOR PLOTTING THE INDIVIDUAL POLICIES 
all_effects_individual <- bind_rows(EV_insu, EV_taxff,EV_waterconserve,EV_build, EV_taxfly)
all_effects_individual <- all_effects_individual %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = case_match(
      variable,
      "gen_trust_cnty_z" ~ "Trust, country",
      "gen_trust_EU_z" ~ "Trust, EU",
      "poli_ori_z" ~ "Political orientation",
      "clim_worry_z" ~ "Climate worry",
      "val_bio1_z" ~ "Biospheric value",
       "imp_exp_fin_z"~"Household\nfinances",
       "imp_exp_econ_z"~"National\neconomy",
       "imp_exp_goal_z"~"Effectiveness",
       "imp_exp_wb_z"~"Well-being",
      "female" ~ "Female",
      "quota_age" ~ "Age",
      "quota_group_educ" ~ "Education",
      "rural" ~ "Rural",
      "hh_inc_z" ~ "Household\nIncome",
      .default = variable
    ),
    group = case_match(
      variable,
      "gen_trust_cnty_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "gen_trust_EU_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "poli_ori_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "clim_worry_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "val_bio1_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "imp_exp_fin_z"~"Policy Evaluations",
      "imp_exp_econ_z"~"Policy Evaluations",
      "imp_exp_goal_z"~"Policy Evaluations",
      "imp_exp_wb_z"~"Policy Evaluations",
      "female" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "quota_age" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "quota_group_educ" ~ "Socio-demographics",
       "rural" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "hh_inc_z" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      .default = variable
    ),
    term_label = factor(term_label, levels = term_levels),
    group = factor(group, levels = rev(group_levels)),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(policy %in% c('Building restrictions','Resilience investment','General adaptation'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap,levels=c(0,1),labels=c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    policy = factor(policy, levels = policy_levels),
    policy_plot = factor(policy, levels = rev(policy_levels))
  )

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FIG 1C: POLICY OBJECTIVES 
overall <- ggplot(all_effects_overall %>% drop_na(any_of('term_label')),
       aes(x = mean_diff, y = term_label,shape = mitig_adap,
           colour = policy, group = policy_plot)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 3.75) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  labs(x = "Expected change in support", y = NULL, colour = "Policy") + # +/- 1 SD
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1.3, 1.3)) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
  scale_colour_manual(values=c("General mitigation" = "#9E3D22", "General adaptation" = "#2B5C8A")) + # "#FECEA3", #C4D8F3
  #scale_colour_manual(values = city_colors) +
  theme_standard() +
  theme(
    legend.position='bottom',
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    )
  )+
  #guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=1))
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      nrow = 1,
      override.aes = list(
        shape = c(17, 16)  # Match order of breaks
      )
    ),
    shape = 'none'
  )

overall
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig1C_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',height=8, width=8)

# FINALISE FIGURE 1 
fig1ABC <- ggarrange(fig1AB,overall,labels=c('','C'),ncol=2)
fig1ABC
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/fig1ABC_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',height=8,width=12)# ,height=8,width=10


# FIG 3
ind <- ggplot(all_effects_individual  %>% drop_na(any_of('term_label')),
       aes(x = mean_diff, y = term_label,shape=mitig_adap,
           colour = policy, group = policy_plot)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 3.75) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
   scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
   scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c(       'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        'Tax beef',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions'))+
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-.75, 1.5)) +
  #scale_colour_manual(values = city_colors) +
  theme_standard() +
  theme(
    legend.position='bottom',
strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    )
  )+
  #guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=1))
  labs(x = "Expected change in support", y = NULL) + # +/- 1 SD make 1 SD for standardised version 

  guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17, 16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)

ind
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig3_ALL_NUM_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',height=8, width=14)

```

```{r alternative indi plot}

attitudes_effs <- all_effects_individual %>% 
  drop_na(any_of('term_label')) %>% 
  filter(variable %in% c('val_bio1_z','clim_worry_z','poli_ori_z','gen_trust_cnty_z','gen_trust_EU_z'))
  

# create alternating bands
bands <- data.frame(
  ymin = seq(0.5, length(unique(attitudes_effs$term_label)) - 0.5, by = 1),
  ymax = seq(1.5, length(unique(attitudes_effs$term_label)) + 0.5, by = 1),
  fill = rep(c("grey90", "white"), length.out = length(unique(attitudes_effs$term_label)))
)

# FIG 3_atts -- need to plot separately and then combine 
ind_atts <- ggplot(attitudes_effs,
  #all_effects_individual %>% drop_na(any_of('term_label')) %>% filter(variable %in% c('val_bio1_z','clim_worry_z','poli_ori_z','gen_trust_cntry_z','gen_trust_E_z')),
       aes(x = mean_diff, y = term_label,shape=mitig_adap,
           colour = policy, group = policy_plot)) +
  geom_rect(data = bands, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = fill),
            xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = .7), size = 3.75) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95),
                position = position_dodge(width = .7), width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
  scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c("Tax fossil fuel profits","Mandatory insulation","Tax flights",
      "Resilience investment","Building restrictions"))+
  
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-.75, 1.5)) +
 
  theme_standard() +
  theme(
   #panel.spacing.y = unit(2, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 1),
    legend.position='bottom',
strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ),
axis.text.y = element_text(size=12)
  )+
  #guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=1))
  labs(x = "", y = NULL) + # Expected change in support +/- 1 SD make 1 SD for standardised version 

  guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17,16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)
ind_atts

imp_effs <- all_effects_individual %>% 
  drop_na(any_of('term_label')) %>% 
  filter(variable %in% c('imp_exp_wb_z','imp_exp_goal_z','imp_exp_econ_z','imp_exp_fin_z'))
  
# create alternating bands
bands <- data.frame(
  ymin = seq(0.5, length(unique(imp_effs$term_label)) - 0.5, by = 1),
  ymax = seq(1.5, length(unique(imp_effs$term_label)) + 0.5, by = 1),
  fill = rep(c("grey90", "white"), length.out = length(unique(imp_effs$term_label)))
)

# FIG 3_atts -- need to plot separately and then combine 
ind_imps <- ggplot(imp_effs,
  #all_effects_individual %>% drop_na(any_of('term_label')) %>% filter(variable %in% c('val_bio1_z','clim_worry_z','poli_ori_z','gen_trust_cntry_z','gen_trust_E_z')),
       aes(x = mean_diff, y = term_label,shape=mitig_adap,
           colour = policy, group = policy_plot)) +
  geom_rect(data = bands, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = fill),
            xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = .7), size = 3.75) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95),
                position = position_dodge(width = .7), width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
  scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c("Tax fossil fuel profits","Mandatory insulation","Tax flights",
      "Resilience investment","Building restrictions"))+
  
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 20))) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-.75, 1.5)) +
 
  theme_standard() +
  theme(
   #panel.spacing.y = unit(2, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 1),
    legend.position='bottom',
strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ),
axis.text.y = element_text(size=12)
  )+
  #guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=1))
  labs(x = "", y = NULL) + # Expected change in support +/- 1 SD make 1 SD for standardised version 

  guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17,16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)
ind_imps


socdem_effs <- all_effects_individual %>%  
  drop_na(any_of('term_label')) %>% 
  filter(variable %in% c('female','quota_age','rural','hh_inc_z','quota_group_educ'))
  
# create alternating bands
bands <- data.frame(
  ymin = seq(0.5, length(unique(socdem_effs$term_label)) - 0.5, by = 1),
  ymax = seq(1.5, length(unique(socdem_effs$term_label)) + 0.5, by = 1),
  fill = rep(c("grey90", "white"), length.out = length(unique(socdem_effs$term_label)))
)

# FIG 3_atts -- need to plot separately and then combine 
socdem_imps <- ggplot(socdem_effs,
  #all_effects_individual %>% drop_na(any_of('term_label')) %>% filter(variable %in% c('val_bio1_z','clim_worry_z','poli_ori_z','gen_trust_cntry_z','gen_trust_E_z')),
       aes(x = mean_diff, y = term_label,shape=mitig_adap,
           colour = policy, group = policy_plot)) +
  geom_rect(data = bands, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = fill),
            xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = .7), size = 3.75) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95),
                position = position_dodge(width = .7), width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
  scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c("Tax fossil fuel profits","Mandatory insulation","Tax flights",
      "Resilience investment","Building restrictions"))+
  
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-.75, 1.5)) +
 
  theme_standard() +
  theme(
   #panel.spacing.y = unit(2, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 1),
    legend.position='bottom',
strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ),
axis.text.y = element_text(size=12)
  )+
  #guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=1))
  labs(x = "Expected change in support", y = NULL) + # +/- 1 SD make 1 SD for standardised version 

  guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17,16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)
socdem_imps

ggarrange(ind_atts,ind_imps,socdem_imps,nrow=3,common.legend = T, legend='bottom')

ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig3_ALL_NUM_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio_separated.png',height = 14, width = 12, dpi = 300)#height=12, width=14)


```

```{r relimp_prep}

preds_z_relimp = c(preds_z, c('cntry')) 

# # UNWEIGHTD
# do_relimp <- function(dat, preds) {
#   f <- reformulate(preds, response = "pol_all")
#   m <- lm(f, data = dat)
#   imp <- calc.relimp(m, type = "lmg", rela = TRUE)
#   data.frame(variable = names(imp$lmg), lmg_rel = imp$lmg, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# }

survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = full_rec_std)

# Weighted version 
do_relimp_weighted <- function(dat, preds) {
  f <- reformulate(preds, response = "pol_all")
  m <- svyglm(f, 
              design = svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = dat),
              family = gaussian(),  
              data = dat)
  imp <- calc.relimp(m, type = "lmg", rela = TRUE)
  data.frame(variable = names(imp$lmg), lmg_rel = imp$lmg, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

```

```{r run_relimp}

results_exc <- full_rec_std %>%
  filter(!is.na(pol_all)) %>%
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
  group_map(~ do_relimp_weighted(.x, preds_z_relimp) %>% mutate(imp_exp_pol_assign = unique(.x$imp_exp_pol_assign)), .keep = TRUE)

table(full_rec_std$gen_trust_EU_z,useNA = 'always')

# Bind results from limp
final_tbl_exc <- bind_rows(results_exc)
View(final_tbl_exc)

final_tbl_exc %>%
  filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == 'Mandatory insulation') %>% 
  filter(variable %in% c('imp_exp_wb_z','imp_exp_goal_z')) %>% 
  summarise(
    sum = sum(lmg_rel)
  )

final_tbl_exc %>%
  filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == 'Tax flights') %>% 
  filter(variable %in%
         # c('imp_exp_goal','imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_econ','imp_exp_fin')
         #c('clim_worry_z','val_bio1_z','poli_ori_z','gen_trust_cnty_z','gen_trust_EU_z')
          #c('hh_inc_z','quota_group_educ','quota_age','rural','female')
           c('cntry')
         ) %>% 
  summarise(
    sum = sum(lmg_rel)
  )

# c('imp_exp_goal','imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_econ','imp_exp_fin')
```

```{r Fig4}
# Plot summary of group decompositions 

policy_levels <- c("Mandatory insulation","Tax fossil fuel profits",
                    "Resilience investment","Building restrictions",
                   "Tax flights")

## PREP PLOT IMPACTS

df_plot_IMP <- final_tbl_exc %>% 
  filter(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% policy_levels) %>% 
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
  summarise(
    " Effectiveness"                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_goal_z"]),
    "Well-being"                      = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_wb_z"]),
     "Household\nfinances"              = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_fin_z"]),
    "National\neconomy"                 = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_econ_z"]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(imp_exp_pol_assign), names_to="variable_type", values_to="lmg_rel") %>%
  mutate(
    variable_label_html = case_when(
      variable_type %in% c("Well-being", "Effectiveness") ~ paste0("",variable_type),
      variable_type %in% c("National economy", "Household finances") ~ paste0("", variable_type), 
      # Everything else as-is
      TRUE ~ variable_type
    ),
    variable_label_html = factor(
      variable_label_html,
      levels = rev(c(
        ' Effectiveness',
        'Well-being',
        'Household\nfinances' ,
        'National\neconomy'
      ))
    ),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Resilience investment','Building restrictions'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap, levels = c(0,1),labels = c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_pol_assign,levels=policy_levels),
    factor_group = 'Policy Evaluations',
    factor_group = factor(factor_group)
  )

## PLOT IMPACTS
shap_imp <- ggplot(df_plot_IMP,
            aes(x = lmg_rel, y = variable_label_html,shape=mitig_adap,
                color = imp_exp_pol_assign, group = factor_group)) + # mitig_adap
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0), size = 4) +
  #scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, .35),labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
   scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c(       'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        #'Tax beef',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions'))+
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(factor_group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  labs(
    x = "", # Percent of Explained Variance
    y = '',
    title = "" #color = guide_legend(title = NULL)
  ) +
  theme_standard()+
  theme(
    legend.position='none',
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=10)
    
  ) + guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17, 16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)

shap_imp
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig4A_FULL_NUM_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',plot=shap_imp,height=5, width=13)


# PREP PLOT ATTITUDES
df_plot_SEP <- final_tbl_exc %>%
   filter(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% policy_levels) %>% 
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
  summarise(
    "Biospheric\nvalue"                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("val_bio1_z")]),
    `Climate worry`                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("clim_worry_z")]),
    "Political\norientation" = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("poli_ori_z")]),
    `Trust, EU` = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("gen_trust_EU_z")]),
    `Trust, country` = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("gen_trust_cnty_z")]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(imp_exp_pol_assign), names_to="variable_type", values_to="lmg_rel") %>%
  mutate(
        variable_label_html = factor(
      variable_type,
      levels = rev(c(
        "Biospheric\nvalue",
        "Climate worry",
        "Political\norientation",
        "Trust, EU",
        "Trust, country"
      ))
    ),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Resilience investment','Building restrictions'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap, levels = c(0,1),labels = c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_pol_assign,levels=policy_levels),
    factor_group = 'Attitudes',
    factor_group = factor(factor_group)
  )

# PLOT ATTITUDES
shap_attitudes <- ggplot(df_plot_SEP,
            aes(x = lmg_rel, y = variable_label_html,shape=mitig_adap,
                color = imp_exp_pol_assign,group=factor_group)) + # mitig_adap
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0), size = 4) +
  #scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, .35),labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
   scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c(       'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        #'Tax beef',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions'))+
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(factor_group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  # guides(color = guide_legend(title = 'Policy')) + 
  labs(
    x = "",#Percent of Explained Variance
    y = '',
    title = "" #color = guide_legend(title = NULL)
  ) +
  theme_standard()+
  theme(
    #legend.position       = "bottom",
    legend.position='none',
strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=10)
    
  ) + guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17, 16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)

shap_attitudes 
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig4B_FULL_NUM_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',plot=shap_attitudes,height=5, width=13)

# PREP PLOT SOCIODEMOS 
df_plot_SOCDEM <- final_tbl_exc %>% 
   filter(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% policy_levels) %>% 
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
  summarise(
    "Gender, Age,\nRural, Income,\nEducation"           = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("female","quota_age","rural_urban",'hh_inc_z')]),
    `Country`                     = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="cntry"]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(imp_exp_pol_assign), names_to="variable_type", values_to="lmg_rel") %>%
  mutate(
    variable_label_html = case_when(
      variable_type %in% c("Benefits: Well-being", "Effectiveness") ~ paste0("",variable_type),
      variable_type %in% c("Costs: Country Economy", "Costs: Household Finances") ~ paste0("", variable_type), 
      TRUE ~ variable_type
    ),
    variable_label_html = factor(
      variable_label_html,
      levels = rev(c(
        "Gender, Age,\nRural, Income,\nEducation",
        "Country"
      ))
    ),
```
