```{r Fig4}
    mitig_adap = ifelse(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Resilience investment','Building restrictions'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap, levels = c(0,1),labels = c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_pol_assign,levels=policy_levels),
    factor_group = 'Sociodemographics',
    factor_group = factor(factor_group)
  )

# PLOT SOCIODEMOS
shap_socdem <- ggplot(df_plot_SOCDEM,
            aes(x = lmg_rel, y = variable_label_html,shape=mitig_adap,
                color = imp_exp_pol_assign, group = factor_group)) + # mitig_adap
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0), size = 4) +
  #scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, .35),labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
   scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c(       'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        #'Tax beef',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions'))+
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(factor_group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  labs(
    x = "Percent of Explained Variance",
    y = '',
    title = "" #color = guide_legend(title = NULL)
  ) +
  theme_standard()+
  theme(
    #legend.position       = "bottom",
    legend.position='bottom',
strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
      angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5,
      size = 12, face = "bold",
      margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
    ),
    strip.background.y = element_rect(
      fill = NA, colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=10)
    
  ) + guides(
  color = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1,
    override.aes = list(shape = c(17, 17, 17, 16, 16))
  ),
  shape = guide_legend(
    nrow = 1
  )
)

shap_socdem
ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig4C_FULL_NUM_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',plot=shap_socdem,height=5, width=13)

# COMBINE COMPONENTS 
shap_full_sep = ggarrange(shap_attitudes,shap_imp,shap_socdem,nrow=3,common.legend = T, legend='bottom')
shap_full_sep

ggsave('figures_r1/final/alessio/Fig4ABC_STD_WEIGHTED_alessio.png',plot=shap_full_sep,height=8, width=14)

## Check correlations 

# # Select columns 
# mitig_only <- full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Mandatory insulation','Tax flights', 'Tax fossil fuel profits'))
# # 
# adap_only <- full_rec_std %>% filter(! imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Mandatory insulation','Tax flights', 'Tax fossil fuel profits'))
# # 
# cols_to_correlate_m <- mitig_only[, c("gen_trust_EU", "gen_trust_cnty",'poli_ori_z')]
# #                                     "imp_exp_goal", "imp_exp_wb",'val_bio1')]
# # 
# cols_to_correlate_a <- adap_only[, c("gen_trust_EU", "gen_trust_cnty",'poli_ori_z')]
# #                                     "imp_exp_goal", "imp_exp_wb",'val_bio1')]
# # 
# # # Calculate correlation matrix
# cor_matrix_m <- cor(cols_to_correlate_m, use = "complete.obs")
# # 
# # # View the correlation matrix
# cor_matrix_m


```

```{r regressions}

# use standardised preds overall and standardised preds spec with country 
# preds_gen = c("gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", 
#           "poli_ori", 
#           "clim_worry",
#           "val_bio1",
#           'cntry',
#           "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc',"quota_group_educ"  
#           ) 
# 
# preds_spec = c("gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", 
#           "poli_ori", 
#           "clim_worry",
#           "val_bio1",
#           'cntry',
#           "imp_exp_wb","imp_exp_goal","imp_exp_econ","imp_exp_fin",
#           "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc',"quota_group_educ" 
#           )


table(wave1_std$quota_group_educ)
table(wave2_std$quota_group_educ)
preds_z_relimp

preds_overall_z_cntry = c(preds_overall_z,c('cntry'))

sub=full_rec_std %>% filter(wave == 2)
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

gen_mtgn = svyglm(reformulate(preds_overall_z_cntry, response = 'gen_sup_mtgn'),
              design = survey_design,
              family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
              data=sub) 

summary(gen_mtgn)
car::vif(gen_mtgn)

gen_adpn = svyglm(reformulate(preds_overall_z_cntry, response = 'gen_sup_adpn'),
              design = survey_design,
              family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
              data=sub) 
summary(gen_adpn)
car::vif(gen_adpn)


sub=wave1_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

mandinsul = svyglm(reformulate(preds_z_relimp, response = 'CNTY_pol_insu'),
                   design = survey_design,
              family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                   data=sub
               ) 
summary(mandinsul)

sub=wave1_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

taxff = svyglm(reformulate(preds_z_relimp, response = 'CNTY_pol_taxff'),
           design = survey_design,
            family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
           data=sub
)
summary(taxff)

sub=wave1_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

taxflight = svyglm(reformulate(preds_z_relimp, response = 'CNTY_pol_taxfly'),
              design = survey_design,
              family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
               data=sub
               ) 
summary(taxflight)

sub=wave2_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

build = svyglm(reformulate(preds_z_relimp, response = 'EU_pol_build'),
               design = survey_design,
              family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
               data=sub
           ) 
summary(build)

sub=wave2_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

resil = svyglm(reformulate(preds_z_relimp, response = 'EU_pol_waterconserve'),
               design = survey_design,
              family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
               data=sub
           ) 
summary(resil)

model <- list(
  'General mitigation' = gen_mtgn,
  'General adaptation' = gen_adpn
  # "Tax fossil fuel profits" = taxff,
  # "Tax flights" = taxflight,
  # "Mandatory insulation" = mandinsul,
  # "Resilience investment" = resil,
  # "Building restrictions" = build
)

oldnames = c('imp_exp_wb','imp_exp_econ','imp_exp_fin','imp_exp_goal',
             'gen_trust_cnty_z','gen_trust_EU_z',
             'poli_ori_z','clim_worry_z','val_bio1_z','female1','quota_age2','quota_age3','quota_age4','quota_age5', 'rural','hh_inc_z','quota_group_educ2','quota_group_educ3','cntry')
coeffs = c('Benefits: Well-being','Costs: National economy','Costs: Household finances',
           'Benefits: Effectiveness',
           'Trust, Country','Trust, EU','Political orientation','Climate worry',
           'Biospheric value','Female','30-39 yr','40-49 yr','50-59','60-74 yr','Rural','Household income','Medium education','High education', 'Country')

# Create named vector
rename_vec <- setNames(coeffs, oldnames)

modelsummary(model,
             #coef_omit = "^cntry",
             coeff_omit = rename_vec,
             estimate  = c(# "{estimate}{stars}",
             #                "{estimate}{stars}",
             #                "{estimate}{stars}",
             #                 "{estimate}{stars}",
             #                 "{estimate}{stars}"
                           "{estimate}{stars}",
                           "{estimate}{stars}"
                           ),# "{estimate} ({std.error})
             output = "Overall Policy Regressions Renamed OVERALLSTD WEIGHTED COUNTRIES.docx")

```

```{r descriptive_stats}

country = 'DE'
#table(w1$cntry)
sub = w1[w1$cntry==country,]
table(sub[sub$quota_gndr == 1,]$quota_age)/nrow(sub)*100


```

```{r fairness_shap}

preds_all_fair <- c("imp_exp_wb","imp_exp_econ", "imp_exp_fin", "imp_exp_goal",
                    'fair_lowinc','fair_mdlcls',
                    "gen_trust_cnty", "gen_trust_EU", 
                    "poli_ori", 
                    "clim_worry", "val_bio1",
                    "female", "quota_age", "quota_group_educ", "rural", 'hh_inc', 'cntry') 
nrow(w2_rec)

adap_pols = c("Resilience investment","Building restrictions")

# Test VIF for each
f_fair <- reformulate(preds_all_fair, response = "pol_all")
m_adap <- lm(f, data = full_rec[full_rec$imp_exp_pol_assign %in% adap_pols,]) # 
vif_values_adap <- car::vif(m_adap)
vif_values_adap

# largest vif is 2.19 

do_relimp <- function(dat) {
  f <- reformulate(preds_all_fair, response = "pol_all")
  m <- lm(f_fair, data = dat)
  imp <- calc.relimp(m, type = "lmg", rela = TRUE)
  data.frame(variable = names(imp$lmg), lmg_rel = imp$lmg, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

# version just for w2
results_exc_fair <- full_rec[full_rec$imp_exp_pol_assign %in% adap_pols,] %>%
  filter(!is.na(pol_all)) %>%
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
  group_map(~ do_relimp(.x) %>% mutate(imp_exp_pol_assign = unique(.x$imp_exp_pol_assign)), .keep = TRUE)
  #group_map(~ do_relimp(.x), .keep = TRUE)

# Bind results from limp
final_tbl_exc_fair <- bind_rows(results_exc_fair)
View(final_tbl_exc_fair)

# Plot 
df_plot <- final_tbl_exc_fair %>% # final_tbl_exc or final_tbl_all
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
    summarise(
      #`**Fairness**` = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("fair_lowinc","fair_mdlcls")]),
    'Low income'            = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="fair_lowinc"]),
    'Middle class'              = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="fair_mdlcls"]),
    `Effectiveness`                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_goal"]),
    'Well-being'                      = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_wb"]),
    'Household finances'              = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_fin"]),
    'National economy'                 = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_econ"]),
    `Biospheric value`                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("val_bio1")]),
    `Climate worry`                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("clim_worry")]),
    `Political orientation` = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("poli_ori")]),
    `Trust, EU` = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("gen_trust_EU")]),
    `Trust, country` = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("gen_trust_cnty")]),
     "Gender, Age,\nRural, Income,\nEducation"           = sum(lmg_rel[variable %in% c("female","quota_age","rural_urban",'hh_inc')]),
    `Country`                     = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="cntry"]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  #pivot_longer(everything(),names_to="variable_type", values_to="lmg_rel") %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(imp_exp_pol_assign), names_to="variable_type", values_to="lmg_rel") %>%
  mutate(
    variable_label_html = case_when(
      variable_type %in% c("Well-being", "Effectiveness") ~ paste0("",variable_type),
      variable_type %in% c("National economy", "Household finances") ~ paste0("", variable_type), 
      # Everything else as-is
      TRUE ~ variable_type
    ),
    variable_label_html = factor(
      variable_label_html,
      levels = rev(c(
        "Low income",
        "Middle class",
        'Effectiveness',
        'Well-being',
        'Household finances' ,
        'National economy',
        "Biospheric value",
        "Climate worry",
        "Political orientation",
        "Trust, EU",
        "Trust, country",
        "Gender, Age,\nRural, Income,\nEducation",
        "Country"
      ))
    ),
    factor_group = case_when(
      variable_type %in% c('Effectiveness','Well-being','Household finances','National economy') ~ 'Policy Evaluations',
      #variable_type %in% c('Household finances','National economy') ~ 'Costs',
      variable_type %in% c('Low income','Middle class') ~ 'Fairness',
      variable_type %in% c('Biospheric value','Climate worry','Political orientation','Trust, EU','Trust, country') ~ 'Attitudes',
       variable_type %in% c('Country',"Gender, Age,\nRural, Income,\nEducation") ~ 'Sociodemographics',
    ),
    factor_group = factor(factor_group)
  )
View(df_plot)

p_fair <- ggplot(df_plot,
            aes(x = lmg_rel, y = variable_label_html,color = imp_exp_pol_assign, group = factor_group)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0), size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c(       'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        'Tax beef',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions'))+
  
  labs(
    x = "Percent of Explained Variance",
    y = NULL,
    title = "" # Shapley Decomposition of Explained Variance - Adaptation Only 
  ) +
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(factor_group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  theme(
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(
                         angle  = 90,     # vertical text (default)
                         hjust  = 0.5,    # centre on the strip
                         vjust  = 0.5,
                         size   = 12,
                         face   = "bold",
                         margin = margin(r = 8)),
panel.spacing.y    = unit(1, "lines"),
strip.background.y = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
    legend.position       = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y    = element_blank(),
axis.text.y = element_text(size=14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=14),
    legend.text = element_text(size=14),
    legend.title = element_text(size=14)
    # element_markdown() will render our HTML arrows & styling
    #axis.text.y           = element_markdown(hjust = 0, lineheight = 0.9)
  ) # +  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
p_fair

ggsave("figures_r1/final/SM_Fairness_ExplainedVar.png",height=13, width=10)


```

```{r sim_impacts}

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Subset the data (mitig vs adap, all rel preds)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

preds_imps_z_overall = c("gen_trust_cnty_z", "gen_trust_EU_z", 
          "poli_ori_z", 
          "clim_worry_z",
          "val_bio1_z",
          "female", "quota_age", "rural",'hh_inc_z',"quota_group_educ"
          ) 

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit the models -- one per policy or mitig/adap index?
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Fit weighted regression model
# model <- svyglm(
#   your_formula ~ predictor1 + predictor2 + ...,
#   design = survey_design,
#   family = gaussian()  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
# )

## set up models - mandatory insulation
sub=full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

# wellbeing
m_impwb_insu <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_wb"),
                    design = survey_design,
                    family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub)
car::vif(m_impwb_insu)
summary(m_impwb_insu)

## mandatory insulation, effectiveness
m_impgoal_insu <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_goal"),
                    design = survey_design,
                    family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub)

car::vif(m_impgoal_insu)
summary(m_impgoal_insu)


## mandatory insulation, national economy
m_impnat_insu <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_econ"),
                    design = survey_design,
                    family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub)

car::vif(m_impnat_insu)

## mandatory insulation, household finances
m_impfin_insu <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_fin"),
                    design = survey_design,
                    family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub)

car::vif(m_impfin_insu)


## CNTY_pol_taxff, 
sub=full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

#wellbeing
m_impwb_taxff <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_wb"),
                  design = survey_design,
                  family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub
                  )

car::vif(m_impwb_taxff)
summary(m_impwb_taxff)

## CNTY_pol_taxff, effectiveness
m_impgoal_taxff <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_goal"),
                  design = survey_design,
                  family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub
             )
car::vif(m_impgoal_taxff)

## CNTY_pol_taxff, national economy
m_impnat_taxff <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_econ"),
                  design = survey_design,
                  family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub
             )
car::vif(m_impnat_taxff)

## CNTY_pol_taxff, household finances
m_impfin_taxff <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_fin"),
                  design = survey_design,
                  family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
                  data = sub
             )
car::vif(m_impfin_taxff)

## CNTY_pol_taxfly, wellbeing
sub=full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_impwb_taxfly <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_wb"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## CNTY_pol_taxfly, effectiveness
m_impgoal_taxfly <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_goal"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## CNTY_pol_taxfly, national economy
m_impnat_taxfly <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_econ"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## CNTY_pol_taxfly, finances
m_impfin_taxfly <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_fin"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )


## EU_pol_waterconserve, wellbeing
sub=full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_impwb_waterconserve <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_wb"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_waterconserve, effectiveness
m_impgoal_waterconserve <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_goal"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_waterconserve, national economy
m_impnat_waterconserve <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_econ"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_waterconserve, finances
m_impfin_waterconserve <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_fin"),
             design = survey_design,
             family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
             data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_build, wellbeing
sub=full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")
survey_design <- svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weights, data = sub)

m_impwb_build <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_wb"),
            design = survey_design,
            family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
            data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_build, effectiveness
m_impgoal_build <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_goal"),
            design = survey_design,
            family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
            data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_build, national economy
m_impnat_build <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_econ"),
            design = survey_design,
            family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
            data = sub
             )

## EU_pol_build, wellbeing
m_impfin_build <- svyglm(reformulate(preds_imps_z_overall, response = "imp_exp_fin"),
            design = survey_design,
            family = gaussian(),  # or family = quasibinomial() for ordinal outcomes
            data = sub
             )

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulate coefficients (one for each model)
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract simulations from the weighted model
library(arm)  # for sim()
set.seed(123)

# Function to simulate coefficients from svyglm
sim_svyglm <- function(model, n.sims = 1000) {
  # Extract coefficients and variance-covariance matrix
  beta_hat <- coef(model)
  V <- vcov(model)
  
  # Simulate from multivariate normal
  library(MASS)
  beta_sims <- mvrnorm(n.sims, mu = beta_hat, Sigma = V)
  
  # Return in a format similar to sim() output
  list(
    coef = beta_sims,
    sigma = summary(model)$dispersion  # For reference
  )
}

# mandatory insulation
sims_impwb_insu <- sim_svyglm(m_impwb_insu, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impgoal_insu <- sim_svyglm(m_impgoal_insu, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impnat_insu <- sim_svyglm(m_impnat_insu, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impfin_insu <- sim_svyglm(m_impfin_insu, n.sims = 1000)

# tax fossil fuel profits
sims_impwb_taxff <- sim_svyglm(m_impwb_taxff, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impgoal_taxff <- sim_svyglm(m_impgoal_taxff, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impnat_taxff <- sim_svyglm(m_impnat_taxff, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impfin_taxff <- sim_svyglm(m_impfin_taxff, n.sims = 1000)

# tax flights
sims_impwb_taxfly <- sim_svyglm(m_impwb_taxfly, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impgoal_taxfly <- sim_svyglm(m_impgoal_taxfly, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impnat_taxfly <- sim_svyglm(m_impnat_taxfly, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impfin_taxfly <- sim_svyglm(m_impfin_taxfly, n.sims = 1000)

# waterconserve
sims_impwb_waterconserve <- sim_svyglm(m_impwb_waterconserve, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impgoal_waterconserve <- sim_svyglm(m_impgoal_waterconserve, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impnat_waterconserve <- sim_svyglm(m_impnat_waterconserve, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impfin_waterconserve <- sim_svyglm(m_impfin_waterconserve, n.sims = 1000)

## build
sims_impwb_build <- sim_svyglm(m_impwb_build, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impgoal_build <- sim_svyglm(m_impgoal_build, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impnat_build <- sim_svyglm(m_impnat_build, n.sims = 1000)
sims_impfin_build <- sim_svyglm(m_impfin_build, n.sims = 1000)

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Apply
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
names(full_rec_std)
# mandatory insulation
EV_impwb_insu <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impwb_insu, sims_impwb_insu, 
                        full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_wb)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')

EV_impgoal_insu <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impgoal_insu, sims_impgoal_insu, 
                                                                       full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_goal)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')

EV_impnat_insu <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impnat_insu, sims_impnat_insu, full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation") %>% dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_econ)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')

EV_impfin_insu <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impfin_insu, sims_impfin_insu, 
                                                                       full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Mandatory insulation") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_fin)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')


# tax fossil fuel profits
EV_impwb_taxff <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impwb_taxff, sims_impwb_taxff, 
                                                                       full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_wb)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')

EV_impgoal_taxff <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impgoal_taxff, sims_impgoal_taxff, 
                                                                       full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_goal)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')

EV_impnat_taxff <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impnat_taxff, sims_impnat_taxff, 
                                                                       full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_econ)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')

EV_impfin_taxff <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impfin_taxff, sims_impfin_taxff, 
                                                                       full_rec_std %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax fossil fuel profits") %>%
                                                                         dplyr::select(all_of(preds_imps_z_overall),weights,imp_exp_fin)),
                         weights_var = 'weights')



# taxfly -- why these different from the tohers?
EV_impwb_taxfly <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impwb_taxfly, sims_impwb_taxfly, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impgoal_taxfly <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impgoal_taxfly, sims_impgoal_taxfly, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impnat_taxfly <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impnat_taxfly, sims_impnat_taxfly, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impfin_taxfly <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impfin_taxfly, sims_impfin_taxfly, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Tax flights")),weights_var='weights')

# waterconserve
EV_impwb_waterconserve <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impwb_waterconserve, sims_impwb_waterconserve, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impgoal_waterconserve <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impgoal_waterconserve, sims_impgoal_waterconserve, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impnat_waterconserve <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impnat_waterconserve, sims_impnat_waterconserve, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impfin_waterconserve <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impfin_waterconserve, sims_impfin_waterconserve, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Resilience investment")),weights_var='weights')

# build 
EV_impwb_build <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impwb_build, sims_impwb_build, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impgoal_build <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impgoal_build, sims_impgoal_build, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impnat_build <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impnat_build, sims_impnat_build, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")),weights_var='weights')

EV_impfin_build <- map_dfr(preds_imps_z_overall, ~simulate_expected_change_std(.x, m_impfin_build, sims_impfin_build, full_rec_std 
             %>% filter(imp_exp_pol_assign == "Building restrictions")),weights_var='weights')


## combine
EV_impwb_insu    <- EV_impwb_insu    %>% mutate(policy = "Mandatory insulation",imp = 'Well-being')
EV_impgoal_insu    <- EV_impgoal_insu    %>% mutate(policy = "Mandatory insulation",imp = 'Effectiveness')
EV_impnat_insu    <- EV_impnat_insu    %>% mutate(policy = "Mandatory insulation",imp = 'National economy')
EV_impfin_insu    <- EV_impfin_insu    %>% mutate(policy = "Mandatory insulation",imp = 'Household finances')

EV_impwb_taxff    <- EV_impwb_taxff    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax fossil fuel profits",imp = 'Well-being')
EV_impgoal_taxff    <- EV_impgoal_taxff    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax fossil fuel profits",imp = 'Effectiveness')
EV_impnat_taxff    <- EV_impnat_taxff    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax fossil fuel profits",imp = 'National economy')
EV_impfin_taxff    <- EV_impfin_taxff    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax fossil fuel profits",imp = 'Household finances')

EV_impwb_taxfly <- EV_impwb_taxfly %>% mutate(policy = "Tax flights",imp = 'Well-being')
EV_impgoal_taxfly    <- EV_impgoal_taxfly    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax flights",imp = 'Effectiveness')
EV_impnat_taxfly  <- EV_impnat_taxfly    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax flights",imp = 'National economy')
EV_impfin_taxfly   <- EV_impfin_taxfly    %>% mutate(policy = "Tax flights",imp = 'Household finances')


EV_impwb_waterconserve <- EV_impwb_waterconserve %>% mutate(policy = "Resilience investment",imp = 'Well-being')
EV_impgoal_waterconserve <- EV_impgoal_waterconserve  %>% mutate(policy = "Resilience investment",imp = 'Effectiveness')
EV_impnat_waterconserve <- EV_impnat_waterconserve  %>% mutate(policy = "Resilience investment",imp = 'National economy')
EV_impfin_waterconserve <- EV_impfin_waterconserve  %>% mutate(policy = "Resilience investment",imp = 'Household finances')

EV_impwb_build <- EV_impwb_build %>% mutate(policy = "Building restrictions",imp = 'Well-being')
EV_impgoal_build <- EV_impgoal_build  %>% mutate(policy = "Building restrictions",imp = 'Effectiveness')
EV_impnat_build <- EV_impnat_build  %>% mutate(policy = "Building restrictions",imp = 'National economy')
EV_impfin_build <- EV_impfin_build  %>% mutate(policy = "Building restrictions",imp = 'Household finances')


#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prep plot
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
term_levels <- c(
  "Trust, country","Trust, EU",
  "Political orientation", 
  "Climate worry",# 
  "Biospheric value",
 # "Country",
  "Female","Age","Education","Rural","Household Income"
)

group_levels <- c("Socio-demographics",
                 'Policy Evaluations',
                  "Attitudes"
                  )
impact_levels =  c("Well-being","Effectiveness","National economy","Household finances")

policy_levels <- c("Mandatory insulation","Tax fossil fuel profits",
                   "Tax flights",
                    "Resilience investment","Building restrictions")#

## now combine them
#all_effects_overall <- bind_rows(EV_impwb_insu, EV_impgoal_insu, EV_impnat_insu, EV_impfin_insu)
#all_effects_overall <- bind_rows(EV_impwb_taxff, EV_impgoal_taxff, EV_impnat_taxff, EV_impfin_taxff)
#all_effects_overall <- bind_rows(EV_impwb_taxfly, EV_impgoal_taxfly, EV_impnat_taxfly, EV_impfin_taxfly)
#all_effects_overall <- bind_rows(EV_impwb_waterconserve, EV_impgoal_waterconserve, EV_impnat_waterconserve, EV_impfin_waterconserve)
all_effects_overall <- bind_rows(EV_impwb_build, EV_impgoal_build, EV_impnat_build, EV_impfin_build)

all_effects_overall <- all_effects_overall %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = case_match(
      variable,
      "gen_trust_cnty_z" ~ "Trust, country",
      "gen_trust_EU_z" ~ "Trust, EU",
      "poli_ori_z" ~ "Political orientation",
      "clim_worry_z" ~ "Climate worry",
      "val_bio1_z" ~ "Biospheric value",
      "female" ~ "Female",
      "quota_age" ~ "Age",
      "quota_group_educ" ~ "Education",
      "rural" ~ "Rural",
      "hh_inc_z" ~ "Household Income",
      .default = variable
    ),
    group = case_match(
      variable,
      "gen_trust_cnty_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "gen_trust_EU_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "poli_ori_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "clim_worry_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "val_bio1_z" ~ "Attitudes",
      "female" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "quota_age" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "quota_group_educ" ~ "Socio-demographics",
       "rural" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      "hh_inc_z" ~ "Socio-demographics",
      .default = variable
    ),
    term_label = factor(term_label, levels = term_levels),
    group = factor(group, levels = rev(group_levels)),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(policy %in% c('Building restrictions','Resilience investment'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap,levels=c(0,1),labels=c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    policy = factor(policy, levels = policy_levels),
    policy_plot = factor(policy, levels = rev(policy_levels)),
    impact = factor(imp, levels = impact_levels),
    impact_plot = factor(impact, rev(impact_levels))
  )

#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot
#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#pdf("plots/04_expected_change_support_by_city.pdf", width = 10, height = 12)
#View(all_effects_overall)
overall <- ggplot(all_effects_overall %>% drop_na(any_of('term_label')),
       aes(x = mean_diff, y = term_label,#shape = mitig_adap,
           colour = impact, group = impact_plot)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 3.75) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower_95, xmax = upper_95),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +
  labs(x = "Expected change in impact perception valence", y = NULL, title = 'Building restrictions') + # +/- 1 SD
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1.1)) +
  #scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
  #                   name = "Policy Type") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c("General mitigation" = "#9E3D22", "General adaptation" = "#2B5C8A")) + # "#FECEA3", #C4D8F3
  #scale_colour_manual(values = city_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position='bottom',
  strip.placement = "outside",
  strip.text.y.left = element_text(
                         angle  = 90,     # vertical text (default)
                         hjust  = 0.5,    # centre on the strip
                         vjust  = 0.5,
                         size   = 12,
                         face   = "bold",
                         margin = margin(r = 8)),
  panel.spacing.y    = unit(1, "lines"),
  strip.background.y = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
  axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(b = 35)),
  axis.title         = element_text(size = 12),
  axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 12),
  legend.title       = element_blank(),
  legend.text        = element_text(size = 10),
  )
  
overall
#ggsave('figures_r1/coeff_overall_clim_worry.png')
ggsave('figures_r1/final/exp_sim_impacts_build_std.png',height=8, width=8)


```

```{r old sim fxs}
# simulate_expected_change_unst <- function(varname, model, sims, data, weights_var = "weights") {
#   
#   # Build baseline - keep numeric variables as numeric!
#   base <- data %>% 
#     summarise(across(
#       -weights_var, 
#       ~ {
#         
#         if (is.numeric(.)) {
#           # Keep as numeric - weighted mean
#           weighted.mean(., w = data[[weights_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
#         } else if (is.factor(.) || is.character(.)) {
#           # For factors - weighted mode
#           tbl <- tapply(data[[weights_var]], ., sum, na.rm = TRUE)
#           modal_value <- names(which.max(tbl))
#           if (is.factor(.)) {
#             factor(modal_value, levels = levels(.))
#           } else {
#             modal_value
#           }
#         } else {
#           mean(., na.rm = TRUE)  # Fallback
#         }
#       }
#     )) 
#   
#   # Determine min/max values for the variable of interest
#   if (is.factor(data[[varname]]) || is.character(data[[varname]])) {
#     var_levels <- levels(factor(data[[varname]]))
#     if (length(var_levels) < 2) return(NULL)
#     
#     min_val <- var_levels[1]
#     max_val <- var_levels[length(var_levels)]
#     
#   } else {
#     # For numeric variables - use weighted quantiles
#     min_val <- weightedQuantile(data[[varname]], w = data[[weights_var]], 
#                                 probs = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
#     max_val <- weightedQuantile(data[[varname]], w = data[[weights_var]], 
#                                 probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
#   }
#   
#   # Create min and max scenarios
#   x_min <- base
#   x_max <- base
#   x_min[[varname]] <- min_val
#   x_max[[varname]] <- max_val
#   
#   # Ensure all factor variables have correct levels matching original data
#   for (col in names(base)) {
#     if (is.factor(data[[col]])) {
#       x_min[[col]] <- factor(x_min[[col]], levels = levels(data[[col]]))
#       x_max[[col]] <- factor(x_max[[col]], levels = levels(data[[col]]))
#     }
#   }
#   
#   # Create predictions using model matrix
#   tryCatch({
#     # Use the model's data to get proper structure
#     X_min <- model.matrix(delete.response(terms(model)), data = x_min)
#     X_max <- model.matrix(delete.response(terms(model)), data = x_max)
#     
#     # Simulate expected outcomes
#     expected_min <- X_min %*% t(sims$coef)
#     expected_max <- X_max %*% t(sims$coef)
#     
#     diffs <- as.vector(expected_max - expected_min)
#     
#     tibble(
#       variable = varname,
#       mean_diff = mean(diffs),
#       lower_95 = quantile(diffs, 0.025),
#       upper_95 = quantile(diffs, 0.975)
#     )
#   }, error = function(e) {
#     warning(paste("Error processing", varname, ":", e$message))
#     return(NULL)
#   })
# }
# 
# # Helper function for weighted quantiles
# weightedQuantile <- function(x, w, probs, na.rm = FALSE) {
#   if (na.rm) {
#     keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(w)
#     x <- x[keep]
#     w <- w[keep]
#   }
#   ord <- order(x)
#   x <- x[ord]
#   w <- w[ord]
#   cumw <- cumsum(w) / sum(w)
#   approx(cumw, x, xout = probs, rule = 2)$y
# }
```

```{R old relimp plot }
# POLICY EVALUATIONS if plotted on own
df_plot <- final_tbl_exc %>% 
  group_by(imp_exp_pol_assign) %>%
  summarise(
    `Effectiveness`                  = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_goal"]),
    `Well-being`                      = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_wb"]),
    `Household finances`              = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_fin"]),
    `National economy`                 = sum(lmg_rel[variable=="imp_exp_econ"]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-c(imp_exp_pol_assign), names_to="variable_type", values_to="lmg_rel") %>%
  mutate(
    variable_label_html = case_when(
      variable_type %in% c("Well-being", "Effectiveness") ~ paste0("",variable_type),
      variable_type %in% c("National economy", "Household finances") ~ paste0("", variable_type), 
      # Everything else as-is
      TRUE ~ variable_type
    ),
    variable_label_html = factor(
      variable_label_html,
      levels = rev(c(
        'Effectiveness',
        'Well-being',
        'Household finances' ,
        'National economy'
        ))
    ),
    mitig_adap = ifelse(imp_exp_pol_assign %in% c('Resilience investment','Building restrictions'),1,0),
    mitig_adap = factor(mitig_adap, levels = c(0,1),labels = c('Mitigation','Adaptation')),
    imp_exp_pol_assign = factor(imp_exp_pol_assign,levels=policy_levels),
    factor_group = case_when(
      variable_type %in% c('Effectiveness','Well-being') ~ 'Benefits',
      variable_type %in% c('National economy','Household finances') ~ 'Costs',
    ),
    factor_group = factor(factor_group)
  )

shap_full <- ggplot(df_plot,
            aes(x = lmg_rel, y = variable_label_html,shape=mitig_adap,
                color = imp_exp_pol_assign, group = factor_group)) + # mitig_adap
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0), size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(17, 16),  # Triangle for general, circle for specific
                     name = "Policy Type") +
   scale_color_manual(values = policy_colors,
                     name = "Policy",
                     breaks = c(       'Tax fossil fuel profits',
                                        'Mandatory insulation',
                                        #'Tax beef',
                                        'Tax flights',
                                        'Resilience investment',
                                        'Building restrictions'))+
  facet_grid(
    rows   = vars(factor_group),
    scales = "free_y",
    space  = "free_y",
    switch = "y",                                   # ← left-hand strips
    labeller = labeller(                            # ← automatic wrapping
      group = label_wrap_gen(width = 17))) +

  labs(
    x = "Percent of Explained Variance",
    y = '',
    title = "" #color = guide_legend(title = NULL)
  ) +
  theme(
      strip.placement = "outside",
      strip.text.y.left = element_text(
                         angle  = 90,     # vertical text (default)
                         hjust  = 0.5,    # centre on the strip
                         vjust  = 0.5,
                         size   = 12,
                         face   = "bold",
                         margin = margin(r = 8)),
  panel.spacing.y    = unit(1, "lines"),
  strip.background.y = element_rect(fill = "grey90", colour = NA),
    legend.position       = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y    = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size=14),
    legend.text = element_text(size=14),
    legend.title = element_text(size=14)
    # element_markdown() will render our HTML arrows & styling
    #axis.text.y           = element_markdown(hjust = 0, lineheight = 0.9)
  ) + # +  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(
      nrow = 2,
      override.aes = list(
        shape = c(17, 17, 17, 16, 16)  # Removed a 17, Match order of breaks
      )
    ),
    shape = guide_legend(nrow = 2)
  )

shap_full 

ggsave('figures_r1/final/Fig4_evaluations_WEIGHTED.png',plot=shap_full,height=5, width=13)
```
